title: Customer Order Frequency
description: Find customers who spent at least $100 in June 2020 AND at least $100 in July 2020.
constraints: customer_id is the primary key of Customers
order_id is the primary key of Orders
product_id is the primary key of Product
order_date is in 2020 — both June and July conditions must be met independently
key_concept: HAVING with two conditional SUM conditions on different date ranges.

hints.L1: Join customers with orders and products. Filter to 2020. Group by customer, check spending by month.
hints.L3: SELECT c.customer_id, c.name FROM Customers c JOIN Orders o ON c.customer_id=o.customer_id JOIN Product p ON o.product_id=p.product_id WHERE YEAR(o.order_date)=2020 GROUP BY c.customer_id HAVING SUM(IF(MONTH(o.order_date)=6, o.quantity*p.price, 0))>=100 AND SUM(IF(MONTH(o.order_date)=7, o.quantity*p.price, 0))>=100

optimal_solution:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.name FROM Customers c
JOIN Orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
JOIN Product p ON o.product_id = p.product_id
WHERE YEAR(o.order_date) = 2020
GROUP BY c.customer_id
HAVING SUM(IF(MONTH(o.order_date)=6, o.quantity*p.price, 0)) >= 100
   AND SUM(IF(MONTH(o.order_date)=7, o.quantity*p.price, 0)) >= 100;
